warning_signs: Taking more than 30 minutes to fall asleep regularly
Waking up multiple times during the night
Feeling tired even after 8+ hours of sleep
Relying on caffeine to stay awake during the day
Falling asleep unintentionally during classes or study
consult_doctor_when: You consistently get less than 5 hours of sleep despite trying to improve
You experience loud snoring or breathing pauses during sleep
You have persistent insomnia lasting more than 3 weeks
You experience sleepwalking or night terrors
Daytime sleepiness significantly affects your daily activities
healthy_indicators: Falling asleep within 15-20 minutes of going to bed
Sleeping through the night with minimal waking
Waking up feeling refreshed and alert

normal_vs_concerning.normal: Some stress before exams or deadlines is natural and can even improve performance
normal_vs_concerning.concerning: Persistent stress that interferes with daily life, relationships, or health needs attention

warning_signs: Feeling overwhelmed most days for more than 2 weeks
Unable to concentrate or make decisions
Withdrawing from friends and activities you used to enjoy
Changes in appetite or sleep patterns due to stress
Physical symptoms like frequent headaches or stomach problems
consult_doctor_when: Stress is causing persistent physical symptoms
You feel hopeless or helpless for more than 2 weeks
You're using alcohol or substances to cope
Stress is significantly impacting your academic performance
You're having thoughts of self-harm or suicide — seek help immediately

healthy_stress_levels.moderate: 4-6: Elevated but manageable with good coping strategies
healthy_stress_levels.high: 7-8: Consider seeking support from a counselor
healthy_stress_levels.critical: 9-10: Please talk to a professional — you deserve support

daily_recommendations.whole_grains: Choose whole grains over refined options

warning_signs: Skipping meals regularly (especially breakfast)
Relying heavily on junk food or processed snacks
Drinking less than 4 glasses of water daily
Eating very late at night frequently
Significant unexplained weight changes
consult_doctor_when: You experience unexplained weight loss or gain
You have persistent digestive problems
You feel faint or dizzy due to irregular eating
You have an unhealthy relationship with food
You suspect food allergies or intolerances
healthy_indicators: Maintaining a healthy relationship with food

recommended_limits.total_leisure: No more than 2-3 hours of leisure screen time per day
recommended_limits.social_media: Limit social media to 30-60 minutes per day
recommended_limits.breaks: Take a break every 20-30 minutes of continuous use
recommended_limits.before_bed: No screens at least 30-60 minutes before sleep

warning_signs: More than 6 hours of non-study screen time daily
Difficulty falling asleep after screen use
Eye strain, headaches, or dry eyes
Feeling anxious when separated from your phone
Neglecting responsibilities due to screen use
consult_doctor_when: You experience persistent eye strain or vision changes
You have chronic headaches related to screen use
You feel unable to control your screen usage
Screen time is causing significant sleep disruption
You experience neck or back pain from prolonged screen use
healthy_indicators: Taking regular breaks during screen use
Using screens intentionally rather than habitually
Maintaining offline hobbies and social connections
No screen use in the hour before bed
